title: Integrate generated Ananta ANSI terminal logo cleanly into CLI/TUI
purpose: Move the useful SVG-derived ANSI terminal logo work from temporary committed artifacts into a clean, tested CLI/TUI banner implementation.

context.source_commit_message: Add ANSI color terminal logos for enhanced display output in temporary directory
context.problem: .tmp/terminal-logo/render_terminal_logo.py was committed to the repository. It is useful as a generator prototype, but .tmp must not be the final location for production code or assets.
important_user_feedback: The terminal logo must be derived from ananta.svg, not hand-drawn ASCII.
The result should be colorful terminal/TUI art.
Previous hand-made ASCII drafts were rejected and must not be restored.

code_review_basis.finding: A Python generator exists at .tmp/terminal-logo/render_terminal_logo.py. It renders ananta.svg-derived PNG into ANSI TrueColor half-block art and monochrome fallback.
The committed location is temporary and unsuitable for production. The code should be moved/refactored or converted into a maintained script/module.

tasks.title: Remove committed .tmp terminal-logo artifacts from final repo state
tasks.summary: Temporary generated files and generator prototypes must not live under .tmp in the committed tree.
acceptance_criteria: No tracked file remains under .tmp/terminal-logo/.
No rendered PNG remains tracked under .tmp/.
The useful generator logic is not lost; it is either moved to scripts/ or converted into a maintained module.
.gitignore prevents future accidental commits of .tmp/ artifacts.

tasks.title: Add or verify .gitignore rules for temporary logo output
tasks.summary: Prevent future accidental commits of generated terminal-logo output.
acceptance_criteria: .tmp/ is ignored unless there is an explicit project reason not to ignore all of it.
If .tmp/ cannot be globally ignored, then .tmp/terminal-logo/ and .tmp/ananta-rendered.png are ignored.
A regression check or documented manual check proves git status stays clean after running the generator.

tasks.title: Move terminal logo generator to maintained scripts path
tasks.summary: Keep the useful SVG-to-ANSI generation path, but move it out of .tmp and make it reusable.
acceptance_criteria: Generator reads ananta.svg by default but accepts --svg and --output-dir.
Generator supports --width 90/120 or arbitrary width.
Generator supports --ansi and --mono outputs.
Generator writes generated artifacts only to an ignored output directory unless explicitly requested.
Generator does not require network access.

tasks.title: Create stable CLI banner module with ANSI and mono fallback
tasks.summary: Expose a simple API for CLI/TUI callers without duplicating banner strings across modules.
acceptance_criteria: agent.cli.banner.get_banner(width=None, color=None, style='auto') exists.
color=False returns mono fallback without ANSI escape sequences.
color=True returns ANSI TrueColor banner when terminal support is assumed/forced.
style='auto' selects large/medium/mono based on width and color.
Assets are stored in a non-temporary project path and are small enough to be reviewable.

tasks.title: Preserve SVG-derived visual identity in generated assets
tasks.summary: The banner must remain derived from ananta.svg and must not regress into hand-drawn ASCII.
acceptance_criteria: Generator documentation states the assets are derived from ananta.svg.
No emoji-based or manually invented snake logo is reintroduced.
ANSI assets use block/half-block characters such as ▀, ▄, █ and ANSI TrueColor.
Mono fallback uses block/half-block or density characters and remains recognizable.
Manual visual review confirms A and snake are recognizable in a dark terminal.

tasks.title: Integrate banner into CLI welcome/help path behind config flag
tasks.summary: Show the logo only where it helps and never break scripts or machine-readable output.
acceptance_criteria: Banner is shown for interactive welcome/status command only, not for JSON/script output.
Environment flag or config can disable banner, e.g. ANANTA_NO_BANNER=1.
NO_COLOR disables ANSI color and uses mono fallback.
Non-TTY output does not emit ANSI escape sequences by default.
Existing CLI tests do not regress.

tasks.title: Add tests for banner selection and ANSI safety
tasks.summary: Tests should verify behavior, not visual beauty.
acceptance_criteria: color=False output contains no ESC character.
color=True output contains ANSI TrueColor escape sequences.
width below threshold selects compact/mono fallback or returns empty if configured.
ANANTA_NO_BANNER disables output.
NO_COLOR disables ANSI color.

tasks.title: Document terminal logo generation and usage
tasks.summary: Document how the banner is generated and when it is shown.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation explains SVG -> PNG -> ANSI halfblock pipeline.
Documentation lists required optional tooling: Pillow and either cairosvg/rsvg/inkscape if needed.
Documentation explains NO_COLOR and ANANTA_NO_BANNER.
Documentation warns not to commit .tmp output.
README mention is short and does not flood the page with huge ANSI output.

definition_of_done: .tmp logo files are no longer tracked.
A maintained generator exists outside .tmp or the generation flow is documented if generator is not committed.
CLI banner module exists with ANSI and mono fallback.
Banner is opt-in/interactive-safe and respects NO_COLOR/ANANTA_NO_BANNER.
Tests cover ANSI/no-ANSI behavior and selection logic.
